original_hypothesis.title: FERC20 burnFrom Does Not Decrement totalSupply Creating Supply Mismatch at Graduation
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: FERC20.burnFrom and _burn reduce _balances[user] but do NOT decrement _totalSupply. During graduation, _openTradingOnUniswap passes _token.data.supply (original totalSupply) and tokenBalance to executeBondingCurveApplication. If the factory uses these to determine agent token allocation, the agent token supply is based on inflated FERC20 supply. Additionally, unwrapToken uses a 1:1 FERC20-to-agent-token mapping, but the pair may only hold lpSupply worth of agent tokens (tokenBalance at graduation), while users collectively hold S-tokenBalance FERC20 tokens. If more than half the supply was bought by users, there are insufficient agent tokens in the pair for all holders to unwrap.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: The burnFrom function explicitly reduces balance without reducing totalSupply, violating the ERC20 conservation invariant. This has downstream effects on graduation economics where supply parameters are derived from stale data.

proof_of_signal.pattern: _balances[user] -= amount without _totalSupply -= amount in both _burn and burnFrom

preconditions: Token graduates through bonding curve (gradThreshold reached)
Users hold more than half of FERC20 supply at graduation
AgentFactoryV3 allocates lpSupply tokens to vault and remainder elsewhere

initial_economic_evidence.suspicious_calculation: _balances[user] -= amount but _totalSupply unchanged; totalSupply() returns inflated value
initial_economic_evidence.manipulation_vector: At graduation, lpSupply=tokenBalance/1e18. If users hold S-tokenBalance > tokenBalance, pair has insufficient agent tokens for unwrap
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: Early unwrappers get their tokens; late unwrappers get nothing (first-come-first-served insolvency)

economic_questions: What does AgentFactoryV3.executeBondingCurveApplication do with totalSupply vs lpSupply tokens?
At typical graduation points, what fraction of supply is held by users vs pair?
Does the inflated totalSupply affect maxTx calculation?
validation_requirements: Verify _totalSupply is not decremented in burnFrom/burn
Trace agent token allocation in AgentFactoryV3 for vault vs other recipients

preliminary_attack_economics.estimated_capital_needed: No attack needed — affects all graduated tokens
preliminary_attack_economics.potential_profit_range: First unwrappers drain pair's agent tokens; latecomers lose entire position
preliminary_attack_economics.estimated_profit_margin: 100% for early callers of unwrapToken vs 100% loss for late callers

market_conditions_analysis.market_impact_assessment: Race condition for unwrap creates panic selling

potential_victim_impact.direct_victims: FERC20 holders who don't unwrap quickly
potential_victim_impact.estimated_impact_per_user: Complete loss of agent token claim
potential_victim_impact.total_value_at_risk: (S - tokenBalance - tokenBalance) worth of agent tokens if more than half supply is with users

investigation_priorities.parameter_analysis: gradThreshold determines token balance ratio at graduation
investigation_priorities.calculation_validation: Verify factory behavior with totalSupply and lpSupply parameters

validation_result.impact_summary: FERC20.burnFrom and _burn reduce _balances[user] without decrementing _totalSupply, violating ERC20 supply conservation. This contributes to a graduation supply mismatch where the pair may hold insufficient agent tokens for all FERC20 holders to unwrap.
validation_result.detailed_analysis:
The hypothesis identifies two related issues: (1) a confirmed code defect in FERC20.burnFrom/_burn that never decrements _totalSupply, and (2) a downstream economic impact during graduation and unwrapping.

**Defect Verification:** Both `_burn` (line ~82-84) and `burnFrom` (line ~86-89) only reduce `_balances[user]` without touching `_totalSupply`. This is an unambiguous violation of the ERC20 supply conservation invariant: after any burn, `totalSupply() > sum(balances)` permanently.

**Graduation Flow:** In `_openTradingOnUniswap`, the call `executeBondingCurveApplication(id, _token.data.supply / 1e18, tokenBalance / 1e18, pairAddress)` passes the original cached supply as totalSupply and the pair's current FERC20 balance as lpSupply. The factory creates totalSupply agent tokens and sends lpSupply worth to pairAddress (the vault). Users collectively hold `S - tokenBalance` FERC20 tokens.

**Unwrap Insolvency:** `unwrapToken` performs a 1:1 burn-and-transfer: for each account, it burns their FERC20 balance and transfers the same amount of agent tokens from the pair. The pair holds `tokenBalance` agent tokens, but users collectively hold `S - tokenBalance` FERC20. If `S - tokenBalance > tokenBalance` (i.e., users bought more than half the supply), there are insufficient agent tokens for all holders.

**burnFrom's role:** While the cached `data.supply` contributes to the mismatch independently, the burnFrom bug compounds the issue: (a) totalSupply() returns permanently wrong values affecting any code or integration that reads it, (b) _maxTxAmount calculated via _updateMaxTx uses _totalSupply and would be inflated if recalculated post-burn, (c) the Transfer event emitted to address(0) in burnFrom signals a proper burn to external observers, but the contract's totalSupply() contradicts this, creating accounting inconsistency.
validation_result.code_evidence_assessment: Lines in FERC20.sol clearly show the defect. The _burn function (lines ~82-84) contains `_balances[user] = _balances[user] - amount;` with no `_totalSupply -= amount;`. The burnFrom function (lines ~86-89) similarly only does `_balances[user] = _balances[user] - amount;` and emits a Transfer event to address(0) but never decrements _totalSupply. Compare with standard ERC20 _burn implementations which always include `_totalSupply -= amount;`.

vulnerable_code_snippets.vulnerability_explanation: Missing `_totalSupply -= amount;` after balance reduction. After any call to _burn, totalSupply() returns a value higher than the actual sum of all balances.
Missing `_totalSupply -= amount;` after balance reduction. Emits Transfer to address(0) suggesting a proper burn, but totalSupply() is never reduced, creating a permanent accounting mismatch.

Passes original cached supply as totalSupply and pair's current balance as lpSupply to create agent tokens. The pair receives lpSupply agent tokens but users hold (supply - tokenBalance) FERC20. After burnFrom (which doesn't reduce totalSupply), the 1:1 unwrap mapping may be insolvent if users bought more than half the supply.

1:1 unwrap burns FERC20 and transfers equal agent tokens from pair. If users collectively hold more FERC20 than the pair holds agent tokens, late unwrappers get nothing. The burnFrom calls here also don't reduce totalSupply, further inflating the phantom supply.

calling_sequence: 1. User calls Bonding.buy(amountIn, tokenAddress) which triggers graduation when newReserveA <= gradThreshold
3. pair.balance() returns tokenBalance (FERC20 remaining in pair)
4. executeBondingCurveApplication creates agent tokens: totalSupply=_token.data.supply/1e18, lpSupply=tokenBalance/1e18, vault=pairAddress
5. pairAddress receives lpSupply worth of agent tokens
6. token_.burnFrom(pairAddress, tokenBalance) burns pair's FERC20 WITHOUT decrementing _totalSupply
7. Users hold S-tokenBalance FERC20 but pair only has tokenBalance agent tokens
8. unwrapToken attempts 1:1 exchange - if S-tokenBalance > tokenBalance, insolvency occurs
execution_path_analysis.vulnerable_code_path: The defect starts in FERC20.burnFrom which never decrements _totalSupply. This is called twice: (1) during _openTradingOnUniswap to burn the pair's FERC20 after agent token creation, and (2) during unwrapToken for each user. The totalSupply() function permanently returns the original minted supply regardless of how many tokens are burned. The graduation process uses the cached _token.data.supply (which equals the original totalSupply) to parameterize agent token creation, and only sends tokenBalance worth of agent tokens to the pair as lpSupply. Since users hold S-tokenBalance FERC20 tokens and the pair only has tokenBalance agent tokens, unwrap insolvency occurs when users purchased more than half the supply before graduation.

config_validation.deployment_evidence: No deployment scripts provided for Bonding or FERC20 directly. The defect is in the FERC20 contract code itself and is not configuration-dependent.

validation_result.attack_scenario_validation: This is a systemic code defect rather than an attack requiring capital. Every graduated token is affected. The scenario: (1) Token launches via Bonding.launchFor with initialSupply S. (2) Users buy tokens through the bonding curve, reducing pair's FERC20 reserve. (3) When gradThreshold is crossed, graduation triggers. (4) executeBondingCurveApplication creates S agent tokens total, sends tokenBalance worth to pair. (5) burnFrom burns pair's FERC20 without reducing totalSupply. (6) Users race to call unwrapToken - early callers get their 1:1 agent tokens, late callers find insufficient agent tokens in pair. The severity depends on gradThreshold: a small threshold means more tokens are in user hands and less in the pair, making insolvency more severe.
validation_result.impact_assessment: HIGH severity. (1) The burnFrom bug is a clear ERC20 standard violation - totalSupply() is permanently inflated after any burn. (2) The graduation flow creates a supply mismatch: agent tokens sent to pair (tokenBalance) may be less than FERC20 held by users (S - tokenBalance). (3) unwrapToken becomes first-come-first-served: early unwrappers get full 1:1 conversion, late unwrappers receive nothing. (4) This affects every graduated token, not just specific scenarios. (5) The totalSupply inflation also affects _maxTxAmount if updateMaxTx is called post-burn, and misleads any external integration reading totalSupply().
recommended_mitigations: Add `_totalSupply -= amount;` to both _burn and burnFrom functions in FERC20.sol

In _openTradingOnUniswap, use the actual circulating supply (totalSupply after burning pair tokens) rather than cached data.supply for the executeBondingCurveApplication totalSupply parameter, or restructure so that the agent token supply sent to pair matches what users actually hold
Consider having unwrapToken check available agent token balance in pair before attempting transfer, to provide a clear error rather than silent failure
Add access control to unwrapToken so users can choose when to unwrap rather than being force-unwrapped by anyone
validation_result.replication_plan: 1. Deploy FERC20 with supply=1000000 and maxTx=100. 2. Verify totalSupply() == 1000000e18. 3. Call burnFrom on an address holding tokens. 4. Verify totalSupply() is unchanged while balanceOf is reduced. 5. For full graduation test: deploy full bonding stack, launch token, buy enough to trigger graduation, verify agent token balance in pair vs total FERC20 held by users, attempt unwrapToken for all holders and observe failure for late callers.
uncertainties: IAgentFactoryV3.executeBondingCurveApplication implementation is not in scope - the exact allocation of agent tokens (how much goes to vault vs other recipients) is unknown, though the interface signature and parameter naming strongly suggest lpSupply goes to vault
The exact gradThreshold value in production deployment is unknown - a very high threshold (pair retains >50% supply) could mitigate the unwrap insolvency, while a low threshold would exacerbate it
Whether the agent token has 18 decimals (assumed in analysis) is not verified from in-scope code
additional_testing_needed: Determine the production gradThreshold value and calculate what fraction of supply typically remains in the pair at graduation
Review the AgentFactoryV3.executeBondingCurveApplication implementation to confirm exact agent token distribution logic
Test end-to-end graduation and unwrap flow with various gradThreshold values to quantify insolvency risk
Verify whether any external protocol or integration reads FERC20.totalSupply() and could be affected by the inflated value
